message:
feat(frankenterm-core): add `--no-auto-start` guard to WeztermClient (ft-dvgzi.1.1)

Prevents the wezterm CLI from silently auto-spawning a daemonized
`wezterm-mux-server` against the user's global mux pid file
(`~/.local/share/wezterm/pid`) when our explicitly configured socket
is briefly unreachable. Without this guard, transient connect-failures
silently redirect commands to the user's interactive mux — a real
isolation breach for any caller using a hermetic socket (sandbox,
container, test fixture, custom domain).

Changes (crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs):

- New `WeztermClient.no_auto_start: bool` field. When true, every spawned
  `wezterm cli ...` invocation is prefixed with `--no-auto-start`.
- `WeztermClient::new()` keeps `no_auto_start = false` — preserves the
  historical behaviour where unparameterised callers can rely on the
  CLI to bring up a mux on demand. No-op for the default constructor.
- `WeztermClient::with_socket(path)` flips the default to
  `no_auto_start = true`. Rationale: a caller who explicitly chose a
  socket path has implicitly committed to that mux; silently failing
  over to a different mux is a bug, not a convenience.
- New builder method `WeztermClient::with_no_auto_start(value: bool)`
  for callers that want to override either default. Returns Self so it
  composes with the existing builder chain.
- Both spawn paths (`run_cli` and the second variant near line 2179)
  conditionally prepend `--no-auto-start` *before* the subcommand —
  argument ordering matters because wezterm's CLI parses
  `--no-auto-start` as a top-level flag, not a subcommand flag.
- Documentation comments on the field, on `with_socket`, and on
  `with_no_auto_start` cite ft-dvgzi.1.1 as the bead capturing the
  hermetic-fixture isolation breach this fixes.

Three untracked files in the working tree (tmp.F6IsX749k7,
tmp.IqA1dweVi5, error_renderer_typed_pane_not_found.actual.json) are
ephemeral test/inventory artifacts and are deliberately not committed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
